result.stdout:
Description:
  A tool to manage the RabbitMQ transport for NServiceBus endpoints

Usage:
  NServiceBus.Transport.RabbitMQ.CommandLine [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  delays    Commands to manage the queues and exchanges for the NServiceBus delay infrastructure
  endpoint  Commands to manage the infrastructure for an NServiceBus endpoint
  queue     Commands to manage individual queues

commands.payload:
Description:
  A tool to manage the RabbitMQ transport for NServiceBus endpoints

Usage:
  NServiceBus.Transport.RabbitMQ.CommandLine [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  delays    Commands to manage the queues and exchanges for the NServiceBus delay infrastructure
  endpoint  Commands to manage the infrastructure for an NServiceBus endpoint
  queue     Commands to manage individual queues

result.stdout:
Description:
  Commands to manage the queues and exchanges for the NServiceBus delay infrastructure

Usage:
  NServiceBus.Transport.RabbitMQ.CommandLine delays [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  create   Create v2 delay infrastructure queues and exchanges
  migrate  Migrate in-flight delayed messages from the v1 delay infrastructure to the v2 delay infrastructure
  verify   Verify broker requirements for using the v2 delay infrastructure

commands.payload:
Description:
  Commands to manage the queues and exchanges for the NServiceBus delay infrastructure

Usage:
  NServiceBus.Transport.RabbitMQ.CommandLine delays [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  create   Create v2 delay infrastructure queues and exchanges
  migrate  Migrate in-flight delayed messages from the v1 delay infrastructure to the v2 delay infrastructure
  verify   Verify broker requirements for using the v2 delay infrastructure

result.stdout:
Description:
  Create v2 delay infrastructure queues and exchanges

Usage:
  NServiceBus.Transport.RabbitMQ.CommandLine delays create [options]

Options:
  -c, --connectionString <connectionString>        Force this command to use the specified connection string
  --connectionStringEnv <connectionStringEnv>      Specifies the environment variable where the connection string can be found. --connectionString, if specified, will take precedence over this option. [default: RabbitMQTransport_ConnectionString]
  --managementApiUrl <managementApiUrl>            Overrides the value inferred from the connection string
  --managementApiUserName <managementApiUserName>  Overrides the value inferred from the connection string. If provided, --managementApiPassword must also be provided or this option will be ignored.
  --managementApiPassword <managementApiPassword>  Overrides the value inferred from the connection string. If provided, --managementApiUserName must also be provided or this option will be ignored.
  --certPath <certPath>                            Set the path to the client certificate file for connecting to the broker
  --certPassphrase <certPassphrase>                The passphrase for client certificate file for when using a client certificate
  --disableCertValidation                          Disable remote certificate validation when connecting to the broker
  --useExternalAuth                                Use the external authorization option when connecting to the broker
  -?, -h, --help                                   Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Migrate in-flight delayed messages from the v1 delay infrastructure to the v2 delay infrastructure

Usage:
  NServiceBus.Transport.RabbitMQ.CommandLine delays migrate [options]

Options:
  -c, --connectionString <connectionString>        Force this command to use the specified connection string
  --connectionStringEnv <connectionStringEnv>      Specifies the environment variable where the connection string can be found. --connectionString, if specified, will take precedence over this option. [default: RabbitMQTransport_ConnectionString]
  --managementApiUrl <managementApiUrl>            Overrides the value inferred from the connection string
  --managementApiUserName <managementApiUserName>  Overrides the value inferred from the connection string. If provided, --managementApiPassword must also be provided or this option will be ignored.
  --managementApiPassword <managementApiPassword>  Overrides the value inferred from the connection string. If provided, --managementApiUserName must also be provided or this option will be ignored.
  --certPath <certPath>                            Set the path to the client certificate file for connecting to the broker
  --certPassphrase <certPassphrase>                The passphrase for client certificate file for when using a client certificate
  --disableCertValidation                          Disable remote certificate validation when connecting to the broker
  --useExternalAuth                                Use the external authorization option when connecting to the broker
  -r, --routingTopology <Conventional|Direct>      Specifies which routing topology to use. [default: Conventional]
  -?, -h, --help                                   Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Commands to manage the infrastructure for an NServiceBus endpoint

Usage:
  NServiceBus.Transport.RabbitMQ.CommandLine endpoint [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  create <endpointName>  Create queues and exchanges for an endpoint

commands.payload:
Description:
  Commands to manage the infrastructure for an NServiceBus endpoint

Usage:
  NServiceBus.Transport.RabbitMQ.CommandLine endpoint [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  create <endpointName>  Create queues and exchanges for an endpoint

result.stdout:
Description:
  Create queues and exchanges for an endpoint

Usage:
  NServiceBus.Transport.RabbitMQ.CommandLine endpoint create <endpointName> [options]

Arguments:
  <endpointName>  The name of the endpoint to create

Options:
  -c, --connectionString <connectionString>          Force this command to use the specified connection string
  --connectionStringEnv <connectionStringEnv>        Specifies the environment variable where the connection string can be found. --connectionString, if specified, will take precedence over this option. [default: RabbitMQTransport_ConnectionString]
  --managementApiUrl <managementApiUrl>              Overrides the value inferred from the connection string
  --managementApiUserName <managementApiUserName>    Overrides the value inferred from the connection string. If provided, --managementApiPassword must also be provided or this option will be ignored.
  --managementApiPassword <managementApiPassword>    Overrides the value inferred from the connection string. If provided, --managementApiUserName must also be provided or this option will be ignored.
  --certPath <certPath>                              Set the path to the client certificate file for connecting to the broker
  --certPassphrase <certPassphrase>                  The passphrase for client certificate file for when using a client certificate
  --disableCertValidation                            Disable remote certificate validation when connecting to the broker
  --useExternalAuth                                  Use the external authorization option when connecting to the broker
  -r, --routingTopology <Conventional|Direct>        Specifies which routing topology to use. [default: Conventional]
  -d, --useDurableEntities                           Specifies if entities should be created as durable
  -t, --queueType <Classic|Quorum>                   Specifies queue type will be used for queue creation [default: Quorum]
  --errorQueueName <errorQueueName>                  Also create an error queue with the specified name
  --auditQueueName <auditQueueName>                  Also create an audit queue with the specified name
  --instanceDiscriminators <instanceDiscriminators>  An optional list of instance discriminators to use when the endpoint needs uniquely addressable instances
  -?, -h, --help                                     Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Create queues and exchanges for an endpoint

Usage:
  NServiceBus.Transport.RabbitMQ.CommandLine endpoint create <endpointName> [options]

Arguments:
  <endpointName>  The name of the endpoint to create

Options:
  -c, --connectionString <connectionString>          Force this command to use the specified connection string
  --connectionStringEnv <connectionStringEnv>        Specifies the environment variable where the connection string can be found. --connectionString, if specified, will take precedence over this option. [default: RabbitMQTransport_ConnectionString]
  --managementApiUrl <managementApiUrl>              Overrides the value inferred from the connection string
  --managementApiUserName <managementApiUserName>    Overrides the value inferred from the connection string. If provided, --managementApiPassword must also be provided or this option will be ignored.
  --managementApiPassword <managementApiPassword>    Overrides the value inferred from the connection string. If provided, --managementApiUserName must also be provided or this option will be ignored.
  --certPath <certPath>                              Set the path to the client certificate file for connecting to the broker
  --certPassphrase <certPassphrase>                  The passphrase for client certificate file for when using a client certificate
  --disableCertValidation                            Disable remote certificate validation when connecting to the broker
  --useExternalAuth                                  Use the external authorization option when connecting to the broker
  -r, --routingTopology <Conventional|Direct>        Specifies which routing topology to use. [default: Conventional]
  -d, --useDurableEntities                           Specifies if entities should be created as durable
  -t, --queueType <Classic|Quorum>                   Specifies queue type will be used for queue creation [default: Quorum]
  --errorQueueName <errorQueueName>                  Also create an error queue with the specified name
  --auditQueueName <auditQueueName>                  Also create an audit queue with the specified name
  --instanceDiscriminators <instanceDiscriminators>  An optional list of instance discriminators to use when the endpoint needs uniquely addressable instances
  -?, -h, --help                                     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Commands to manage individual queues

Usage:
  NServiceBus.Transport.RabbitMQ.CommandLine queue [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  migrate-to-quorum <queueName>        Migrate an existing classic queue to a quorum queue
  validate-delivery-limit <queueName>  Validate that the queue has an unlimited delivery limit

result.stdout:
Description:
  Migrate an existing classic queue to a quorum queue

Usage:
  NServiceBus.Transport.RabbitMQ.CommandLine queue migrate-to-quorum <queueName> [options]

Arguments:
  <queueName>  The name of the classic queue to migrate to a quorum queue

Options:
  -c, --connectionString <connectionString>        Force this command to use the specified connection string
  --connectionStringEnv <connectionStringEnv>      Specifies the environment variable where the connection string can be found. --connectionString, if specified, will take precedence over this option. [default: RabbitMQTransport_ConnectionString]
  --managementApiUrl <managementApiUrl>            Overrides the value inferred from the connection string
  --managementApiUserName <managementApiUserName>  Overrides the value inferred from the connection string. If provided, --managementApiPassword must also be provided or this option will be ignored.
  --managementApiPassword <managementApiPassword>  Overrides the value inferred from the connection string. If provided, --managementApiUserName must also be provided or this option will be ignored.
  --certPath <certPath>                            Set the path to the client certificate file for connecting to the broker
  --certPassphrase <certPassphrase>                The passphrase for client certificate file for when using a client certificate
  --disableCertValidation                          Disable remote certificate validation when connecting to the broker
  --useExternalAuth                                Use the external authorization option when connecting to the broker
  -?, -h, --help                                   Show help and usage information
